2604.07791.title: SEARL: Joint Optimization of Policy and Tool Graph Memory for Self-Evolving Agents
2604.07791.paper_summary: Recent advances in Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) have demonstrated significant potential in single-turn reasoning tasks. With the paradigm shift toward self-evolving agentic learning, models are increasingly expected to learn from trajectories by synthesizing tools or accumulating explicit experiences. However, prevailing methods typically rely on large-scale LLMs or multi-agent frameworks, which hinder their deployment in resource-constrained environments. The inherent sparsity of outcome-based rewards also poses a substantial challenge, as agents typically receive feedback only upon completion of tasks. To address these limitations, we introduce a Tool-Memory based self-evolving agentic framework SEARL. Unlike approaches that directly utilize interaction experiences, our method constructs a structured experience memory that integrates planning with execution. This provides a novel state abstraction that facilitates generalization across analogous contexts, such as tool reuse. Consequently, agents extract explicit knowledge from historical data while leveraging inter-trajectory correlations to densify reward signals. We evaluate our framework on knowledge reasoning and mathematics tasks, demonstrating its effectiveness in achieving more practical and efficient learning.
2604.07791.paper_summary_zh: 最近在可驗證獎勵的強化學習（RLVR）方面的進展顯示出在單回合推理任務中具有顯著潛力。<lb/>隨著向自我演化的代理學習的範式轉變，模型越來越被期望通過合成工具或積累明確的經驗來從軌跡中學習。<lb/>然而，現有的方法通常依賴於大規模的LLM或多代理框架，這限制了它們在資源受限環境中的部署。<lb/>基於結果的獎勵的固有稀疏性也構成了實質性的挑戰，因為代理通常僅在任務完成後才會收到反饋。<lb/>為了解決這些限制，我們提出了一種基於工具記憶的自我演化代理框架SEARL。<lb/>與直接利用互動經驗的方法不同，我們的方法構建了一個結構化的經驗記憶，將規劃與執行整合在一起。<lb/>這提供了一種新穎的狀態抽象，有助於在類似上下文中進行泛化，例如工具重用。<lb/>因此，代理從歷史數據中提取明確的知識，同時利用軌跡間的相關性來密集獎勵信號。<lb/>我們在知識推理和數學任務上評估了我們的框架，證明其在實現更實用和高效的學習方面的有效性。
2604.07791.author: Xinshun Feng et.al.
2604.07791.authors: Xinshun Feng, Xinhao Song, Lijun Li, Gongshen Liu, Jing Shao
